raw_comment:
Newspapers try to offer headlines that grab the reader's attention.  They tend to be shorter and have a zing -- something that the reader would not expect.  Maybe it surprises the reader with either surprise or shock.
Always the headlines come from evidence in the graph and not other information or opinion.  
What do you propose?
ace_sentences: These headlines grab the reader's attention.
These headlines tend to be shorter.
A zing is something that the reader would not expect.
A headline maybe surprises the reader.
A headline maybe surprises the reader with surprise.
A headline maybe surprises the reader with shock.
Always the headlines come from evidence in the graph.
The headlines do not come from other information.
The headlines do not come from opinion.
